description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade showcases a bay window arrangement, providing architectural interest and natural light. A large, partially enclosed porch with a translucent roof extends from one side, supported by wooden posts and surrounded by wooden railings. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige color, contrasting with the dark brown trim and decking that wraps around the building. The rear and side elevations are simpler, with fewer windows and a continuous shingled roofline. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for suburban or residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a gray gable roof, bay window front, and a large side porch with a translucent roof and wooden railings.
description_view_0: The view highlights the translucent-roofed porch at the front, the bay window, and the wraparound wooden deck.
description_view_1: This side view shows the full length of the gable roof and the porch extending from the left, with minimal window detail.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain wall with no windows and the continuation of the deck around the building.
description_view_3: The front facade is visible, featuring the bay window, main entrance, and the porch extending to the right.
attribute_window_arrangement: bay window on front facade with asymmetrical side windows
